title: General Aptitude - Verbal Aptitude - Statement Sufficiency

questions.question:
Based on the given statements, select the most appropriate option to solve the given question.

If two floors in a certain building are \( 9 \) feet apart, how many steps are there in a set of stairs that extends from the first floor to the second floor of the building?

Statements:

  1. Each step is \( 3/4 \) foot high.
  2. Each step is \( 1 \) foot wide. 


GATE CSE 2015 Set 1 | Question: GA-4
options: Statements I alone is sufficient, but statement II alone is not sufficient.
Statements II alone is sufficient, but statement I alone is not sufficient.
Both statements together are sufficient, but neither statement alone is sufficient.
Statements I and II together are not sufficient.
questions.correct_answer: Statements I alone is sufficient, but statement II alone is not sufficient.

questions.question:
Based on the given statements, select the most appropriate option to solve the given question.

What will be the total weight of \( 10 \) poles each of same weight?

Statements:

  1. One fourth of the weight of the pole is \( 5\;\text{Kg} \).
  2. The total weight of these poles is \( 160\;\text{Kg} \) more than the total weight of two poles.


GATE CSE 2015 Set 2 | Question: GA-5
options: Either I or II alone is sufficient.
Both statements I and II together are not sufficient.
questions.correct_answer: Either I or II alone is sufficient.
